transcript: Speaker A: Stop saying 'I'm fine, thanks', 'I don't know', 'You're welcome'. It's very basic. I used to speak the same way. Then my friend suggested practicing with Loora. Let me show you. Speaker B: Sorry, my English is not good. Speaker C: You should practice everyday. Speaker B: But I don't have anyone to practice with. Speaker C: That's exactly what I'm here for. I'm Loora, your AI English tutor. Speaker B: I can speak with you? Speaker C: Yes, I'll chat with you, give you instant feedback on your pronunciation, and help you get better with every conversation. Speaker B: But I make a lot of mistakes. Speaker C: You don't have to worry about making mistakes because I'll guide you through them. Speaker B: Sounds good! Speaker C: Let's practice together every day and you'll see how quickly your English improves.
on_screen_text: STOP SAYING. I'm fine, thanks. I don't know. You're welcome. It's very basic. I used to speak the same way. Then my friend suggested practicing with Loora. Let me show you. Sorry, my English is not good. You should practice everyday. But I don't have anyone to practice with. That's exactly what I'm here for. I'm Loora, your AI English tutor. I can speak with you? Yes, I'll chat with you, give you instant feedback on your pronunciation, and help you get better with every conversation. But I make a lot of mistakes. You don't have to worry about making mistakes because I'll guide you through them. Sounds good! Let's practice together every day and you'll see how quickly your English improves. Loora.
summary: The ad introduces Loora, an AI English tutoring app, as an alternative to basic English phrases. It showcases a split-screen conversation between a learner and the AI tutor, highlighting features like real-time pronunciation feedback and mistake correction. The ad encourages daily practice to quickly improve English speaking skills.
